Combat sport events in 2023
ONE Friday Fights 46: Tawanchai vs. Superbon
The poster for ONE Friday Fights 46: Tawanchai vs. Superbon
PromotionONE Championship
DateDecember 22, 2023 (2023-12-22)
VenueLumpinee Boxing Stadium
CityBangkok, Thailand
Event chronology
ONE Friday Fights 45: Otop vs. Şen ONE Friday Fights 46: Tawanchai vs. Superbon ONE Fight Night 18: Gasanov vs. Oh

ONE Friday Fights 46: Tawanchai vs. Superbon (also known as ONE Lumpinee 46) was a combat sport event produced by ONE Championship that took place on December 22, 2023, at Lumpinee Boxing Stadium in Bangkok, Thailand.[1]

Background

[edit]

A ONE Featherweight Muay Thai World Championship bout between current champion Tawanchai P.K.Saenchai and former ONE Featherweight Kickboxing World Champion Superbon Singha Mawynn headlined the event.[2] The pairing was previously scheduled to headline at ONE Fight Night 15, but Superbon was forced to pulled out due to a leg injury.[3] The second was scheduled to headline at ONE Fight Night 17, but Tawanchai withdrew from the fight due to being hospitalized with a viral infection.[4]

A ONE Strawweight Muay Thai World Championship title unification bout between current champion Joseph Lasiri and former champion/current interim title holder Prajanchai P.K.Saenchai took place at the event.[5] The pairing previously met at ONE 157, with Lasiri winning the title by TKO (retirement) in round three.[6]

An interim ONE Women's Atomweight Kickboxing World Championship bout between Phetjeeja Lukjaoporongtom and former Glory Women's Super Bantamweight champion Anissa Meksen also took place at the event.[7]

Results

[edit]
Fight card (PPV at Watch ONE)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Featherweight Muay Thai Tawanchai P.K.Saenchai (c) def. Superbon Singha Mawynn Decision (majority) 5 3:00 [a]
Strawweight Muay Thai Prajanchai P.K.Saenchai (ic) def. Joseph Lasiri (c) KO (elbow) 1 1:28 [b]
Women's Atomweight Kickboxing Phetjeeja Lukjaoporongtom def. Anissa Meksen Decision (unanimous) 5 3:00 [c]
Bantamweight Muay Thai Nico Carrillo def. Nong-O Hama KO (elbow) 2 1:28
Catchweight (140 lbs) Muay Thai Saeksan Or. Kwanmuang def. River Daz Decision (split) 3 3:00
Preliminary card (YouTube / Watch ONE)
Catchweight (136 lbs) Muay Thai Nabil Anane def. Muangthai P.K.Saenchai Decision (unanimous) 3 3:00
Bantamweight Muay Thai Kulabdam Sor.Jor.Piek-U-Thai def. Fariya Aminipour KO (punch to the body) 1 2:51
Flyweight Muay Thai Jawsuayai Sor.Dechaphan def. Phetsukumvit Boybangna Decision (majority) 3 3:00
Bantamweight Muay Thai Suablack Tor.Pran49 def. Craig Coakley KO (punch to the body) 1 2:15
Catchweight (132 lbs) Muay Thai Chorfah Tor.Sangtiennoi def. Eisaku Ogasawara KO (punch) 2 0:57
Catchweight (132 lbs) Muay Thai Suriyanlek Por.Yenying def. Yodkritsada Sor.Sommai KO (punch) 2 0:15
  1. ^ For the ONE Featherweight Muay Thai World Championship.Originally a unanimous decision win for Tawanchai; the scorecard was updated.
  2. ^ For the ONE Strawweight Muay Thai World Championship.
  3. ^ For the interim ONE Women's Atomweight Kickboxing World Championship.

[8]

Bonus awards

[edit]

The following fighters were awarded bonuses:

  • Performance of the Night ($50,000): Prajanchai P.K.Saenchai
  • Performance of the Night ($10,000): Kulabdam Sor.Jor.Piek-U-Thai, Jawsuayai Sor.Dechaphan, Phetsukumvit Boybangna, Suablack Tor.Pran49, Chorfah Tor.Sangtiennoi and Suriyanlek Por.Yenying
